title: Backlog execution discipline: sequential queue, version atomicity, test gate hygiene
context: v0.4.0→v0.4.1 session: 16 tasks (INFRA×5, SWARM×2, CLI×3, TEST×1, BUG×1, SHELL×1, DOC×3) completed in ~3 sessions with dashboard-driven scoring and sequential pick-off.
findings: Sequential task queue with heat-scored priority (score = 10P + 25S + 100H) consistently picks the right next task — no context-switching waste
Dashboard-as-commit-trail (each task completion = 2 commits: work + dashboard) creates auditable progress and prevents stale tracking
Version bump + tag + push must be atomic — stale version strings (0.3.2 displayed when v0.4.0 was tagged) confuse downstream and erode trust
Pre-existing test failures (12 gh-auth / aibash-help) are environmental, not code bugs, but they erode CI confidence — must be gated behind Skip-if conditions
DOC-002 was auto-resolved by CLI-001 (show_help rewrite) — task system correctly identifies when upstream work subsumes downstream tasks
Session prune bug (BUG-001) was only caught because test001_spec exercised the code path — tests find real bugs even in utility scripts
recommendations: Gate environmental test failures behind Skip-if before v0.5
Add CHANGELOG.md to capture version-to-version deltas
Continue dashboard-driven execution for v0.5 backlog
Version bump should be the LAST commit before tag, not mixed with other work
